title: Legacy IT overhauled: Akamai and DCT target media, healthcare cloud
summary: Akamai Technologies has announced that Digital Convergence Technologies (DCT) has joined its Partner Connect Program to simplify cloud modernization for mid-market and enterprise clients. This partnership will focus on refactoring, architecture modernization, and managed services, particularly targeting the media, healthcare, and fintech sectors. The collaboration aims to provide cost optimization and compliance-accredited solutions (HIPAA, ISO 27001, HITRUST) while moving away from traditional "lift and shift" cloud migration methods.

title: F5 CEO On ‘The Most Exciting Time’ In Two Decades As AI Accelerates App Delivery, Security: Exclusive
summary: F5 President and CEO François Locoh-Donou discusses how AI is dramatically changing application delivery and security, creating a significant opportunity for F5 and its partners. He emphasizes the importance of F5's updated Application Delivery and Security Platform (ADSP) for consolidating services across hybrid and multi-cloud environments, and highlights the growing need for API security and AI model protection. Locoh-Donou encourages partners to leverage refresh cycles to expand into new offerings like AI data delivery and to drive the adoption of F5's comprehensive platform.

title: Digital Convergence Technologies (DCT) Joins Akamai Partner Connect Program
summary: Digital Convergence Technologies (DCT) has joined the Akamai Partner Connect Program, aiming to simplify complex IT challenges and provide cost-effective cloud modernization for mid-market and enterprise clients. This partnership leverages Akamai's distributed infrastructure and developer-friendly cloud with DCT’s expertise in refactoring and architecture modernization. The collaboration will support customers in media, healthcare, and fintech verticals with digital modernization, cost optimization, and specialized managed services, enabling them to transition from legacy systems to scalable cloud architectures.

title: Will Akamai’s US$200 Million AI Compute Deal and GPU Push Change Akamai Technologies' (AKAM) Narrative?
summary: Akamai Technologies has secured a four-year, US$200 million AI compute services agreement and announced plans for a multi-thousand NVIDIA Blackwell GPU cluster. These moves indicate Akamai's strategic repositioning towards an AI and security-centric cloud provider, moving away from its traditional CDN business. While the deal strengthens its cloud infrastructure, it also introduces risks such as margin pressure and dependency on large customers, prompting varied analyst outlooks on its future performance and valuation.

title: Mani Sundaram Sells 3,186 Shares of Akamai Technologies (NASDAQ:AKAM) Stock

title: Akamai at Morgan Stanley Conference: Strategic Shift to Cloud and AI
summary: Akamai Technologies Inc. presented its strategic shift towards cloud and edge AI inference at the Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference 2026. CFO Ed McGowan detailed the growth of Akamai's public cloud business, the innovation surrounding the Akamai Inference Cloud leveraging GPUs, and the rapid expansion of API Security. Despite margin pressures from increased co-location and depreciation costs, the company remains optimistic about its future growth, driven by customer demand and strategic investments.

title: According to the latest cooperation agreement, Akamai Technologies Inc's clients will deploy a large computing cluster containing thousands of Nvidia Blackwell architecture GPUs.
summary: Akamai Technologies Inc.'s clients are set to deploy a significant computing cluster powered by thousands of Nvidia Blackwell architecture GPUs, following a new cooperation agreement. This strategic move highlights the company's focus on high-performance computing and artificial intelligence infrastructure. The deployment underscores Akamai's commitment to advancing its capabilities in these critical technological domains.

title: Akamai Technologies Announces $200 Million AI Compute Service Agreement with Major U.S. Tech Company
summary: Akamai Technologies has announced a four-year, $200 million agreement with a leading U.S. tech company for high-performance AI compute services, utilizing a large NVIDIA Blackwell GPU cluster. This deal validates Akamai's strategy for a global AI infrastructure and highlights the increasing demand for its integrated AI platform and cloud services. The company's COO emphasized that this agreement provides a strong foundation for AI workloads, ensuring predictable and reliable performance for end-users.

title: Akamai to run huge NVIDIA AI cluster for major US tech client in $200M deal
summary: Akamai Technologies has secured a four-year, $200 million service agreement to host a multi-thousand NVIDIA Blackwell GPU cluster for a major U.S. AI company. This deal highlights Akamai's integrated AI development platform, leveraging high-density data centers, AI-optimized Ethernet, NVMe-over-Fabric storage, and its expanded global IaaS footprint of 41 datacenters. The agreement reinforces Akamai's strategy around large-scale, GPU-powered AI infrastructure and follows previous AI-focused expansions.
